meta.title: Wild Card vs Packers: Drive Survival -> Execution
meta.subtitle: Five decisive fourth-quarter Bears offensive plays showing how one survival rep enabled the execution cascade.
meta.game: Chicago Bears vs Green Bay Packers
meta.default_panel_text: Click a timestamp to see how the drive survived and turned into execution.

plays.detail_text: Williams finds Rome Odunze for 27 yards on 4th & 8, keeping the drive alive. The play lifts Chicago to 11.8% post-play win probability and becomes the survival rep enabling the comeback.
plays.significance_note: This was the survival play. Without it, the drive ends immediately.

plays.detail_text: Williams hits Olamide Zaccheaus for an 8-yard touchdown over the middle. The play raises Chicago to 20.4% post-play win probability and shifts the drive from survival into execution.
plays.significance_note: The offense is now operating within structure again.

plays.detail_text: Williams finds Colston Loveland for 12 yards to stabilize the drive. The completion raises Chicago to 43.6% post-play win probability and keeps the offense on schedule moving forward.
plays.significance_note: This play sustains rhythm and keeps the drive on schedule.

plays.detail_text: Williams gets the ball to D’Andre Swift for 23 yards into scoring range. The play raises Chicago to 56.8% post-play win probability and flips the field before the decisive scoring shot.
plays.significance_note: This gain flips field position and pushes Chicago into scoring range.

plays.detail_text: Williams hits DJ Moore for a 25-yard touchdown to complete the comeback. The play raises Chicago to 75.4% post-play win probability, but it only exists because the drive first survived.
plays.significance_note: This is the decisive execution play that completes the comeback.
